codeReview.summary: The rerun fix fully addresses the prior blocking gap. Compared with the failed `0129357` implementation, the final-expression `Ok(...)`, `Err(...)`, and `Some(...)` branches in `gen_final_expr_as_return` now route through the same `emit_return_cleanup` flow used by explicit returns (`lib/codegen.ml:2158`, `2178`, `2207`), the final-expression `None` and bare-identifier paths still emit nested cleanup (`lib/codegen.ml:2218-2237`), and the generic final-expression fallback now also uses `emit_return_cleanup` (`lib/codegen.ml:2268`). The added ownership codegen tests cover the previously missing final-expression guard-suppression and nested-cleanup cases (`test/test_codegen_ownership.ml:1940-1954`). I did not find any remaining high-confidence correctness bug in the combined original-plus-fix behavior for the reviewed return/loop cleanup paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The compiler-worker skill still says ownership-sensitive work must record one negative compile path plus one positive runtime path, but this handoff reports `followedProcedure: true` while documenting only positive compile/runtime validation for the feature.
